topics.title: Europe faces record June heat
topics.summary: A major heatwave is pushing parts of Europe into record June temperatures, disrupting transport and raising health concerns. France, Spain, Italy, Belgium and the UK are among the affected areas.
topics.discussionPrompt: It feels like summers are getting harder to plan around. Would you rather live somewhere with harsh winters, or somewhere that keeps getting these intense heatwaves?

topics.title: UN nuclear inspectors expected to visit Iran sites
topics.summary: The UN nuclear agency says inspectors are expected to visit Iranian nuclear sites under an interim Iran-US deal, while Iranian officials say details depend on a final agreement and sanctions action.
topics.discussionPrompt: International deals always seem to come down to trust and timing. Do you think inspections actually calm people down, or do they mostly just create new arguments?

topics.title: Tech stocks steady after AI-driven selloff
topics.summary: Global markets showed signs of stabilizing after a sharp tech selloff, with investors watching AI-related valuations, chip stocks and Micron earnings. Oil prices fell and the US dollar strengthened.
topics.discussionPrompt: AI has become such a big part of the stock market story. Do you think investors are being smart about it, or is everyone getting a bit carried away?

topics.title: North Korea says new warship is in service
topics.summary: North Korean state media says Kim Jong Un has placed a new warship into service and claimed progress toward a nuclear-armed navy. The announcement adds to regional security concerns.
topics.discussionPrompt: Some countries make big military announcements partly for show. How do you tell the difference between real danger and political theatre?
